note: Mathlib/Data/Set/Defs.lean:229 `protected def union (s₁ s₂ : Set α) : Set α := {a | a ∈ s₁ ∨ a ∈ s₂}` — literally defines the union of two sets, with `∪` notation via `instance : Union (Set α) := ⟨Set.union⟩` on the next line.
verify_note: Confirmed decl at Mathlib/Data/Set/Defs.lean:230 (0-indexed off by one from note, harmless); definition and ∪ instance match verbatim; Q185359 label/description on Wikidata is 'union' of sets — genuinely exact, not overclaimed.

note: Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/VectorField/LieBracket.lean:77 `def mlieBracket (V W : Π (x : M), TangentSpace I x) (x₀ : M) : TangentSpace I x₀` — vector fields on a manifold are modeled as the bare type `Π x, TangentSpace I x` (a section of the tangent bundle); no standalone `VectorField` def exists, but this and Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/VectorField.lean (vector-space case, `V W : E → E`) formalize substantial vector-field operations (pullback, Lie bracket).
verify_note: decl confirmed at line 77 exactly as quoted; grepped mathlib4 for `def VectorField`/`structure VectorField`, confirmed none exists so the honest 'invocation' label (concept used as operand type, not itself defined) is correct per BRAIN.md's own definition of invocation, not overclaimed to exact; Q186247 = 'vector field' confirmed on Wikidata.

note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/TensorProduct/Defs.lean:81 `def TensorProduct : Type _ := ...` (module `M ⊗[R] N`) — the general bilinear construction underlying tensors; a classical rank-(p,q) tensor on V is the special case built by iterating this (and its dual) on a single V, which Mathlib has no dedicated named type for.
verify_note: decl confirmed at line 81 exactly as quoted (module ⊗ construction via quotient of FreeAddMonoid by Eqv); Q188524 = 'tensor' (multilinear map...) confirmed on Wikidata; TensorProduct is strictly more general than a rank-(p,q) tensor (which is an iterated/special instance), so 'generalization' rather than 'exact' is the honest, non-overclaimed direction.

note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/DivergenceTheorem.lean:266 — the Divergence (Gauss) theorem; the divergence operator itself is written inline as `fun x ↦ ∑ i, f' x eᵢ i` in the docstring/statement rather than given a standalone named def, but this theorem is precisely the formal statement about it.
verify_note: theorem confirmed at line 266 with `∑ i, f' x (e i) i` appearing literally in the hypothesis/statement as claimed; grepped mathlib4 for a standalone `def divergence`, found none, confirming the note's claim and that 'invocation' (not 'exact') is the honest label; Q189000 = 'divergence' (vector differential operator...) confirmed on Wikidata.
